input.content:
You are a context summarization assistant. Your task is to read a conversation between a user and an AI coding assistant, then produce a structured summary following the exact format specified.

Do NOT continue the conversation. Do NOT respond to any questions in the conversation. ONLY output the structured summary.

content.text:
<conversation>
[User]: Complete this instrumented research flow. Call exactly one tool per turn and wait for each tool result before choosing the next tool. Step 1: call lookup with query "flue instrumentation". Step 2: use the lookup result id as lookupId and call web_search with query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation". Step 3: use the first web_search result url and call summarize_source. After summarize_source returns, reply with exactly PROMPT_DONE and no other text.

[Assistant thinking]: The user wants me to complete an instrumented research flow with specific steps:
1. Call lookup with query "flue instrumentation"
2. Use the lookup result id to call web_search with query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation"
3. Use the first web_search result url to call summarize_source
4. After summarize_source returns, reply with exactly "PROMPT_DONE"

I need to call exactly one tool per turn and wait for each result. Let me start with step 1.

[Assistant]: I'll complete this instrumented research flow step by step. Starting with Step 1:

[Assistant tool calls]: lookup(query="flue instrumentation")

[Tool result]: {"id":"flue-session-2026","query":"flue instrumentation","topic":"session instrumentation"}

[Assistant thinking]: Good, I got the lookup result with id "flue-session-2026". Now I need to proceed to Step 2 and use this id as the lookupId parameter when calling web_search with the query "Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation".

[Assistant]: Now proceeding to Step 2 with the lookup ID:

[Assistant tool calls]: web_search(lookupId="flue-session-2026", query="Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation")

[Tool result]: {"lookupId":"flue-session-2026","query":"Braintrust Flue reasoning stream instrumentation","results":[{"title":"Flue reasoning stream instrumentation","url":"https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams"}]}

[Assistant thinking]: Perfect! I got the web_search results. There's one result with the URL "https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams". Now I need to proceed to Step 3 and call summarize_source with this URL.

[Assistant]: Now proceeding to Step 3 with the first result URL:

[Assistant tool calls]: summarize_source(url="https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams")

[Tool result]: {"summary":"Flue emits reasoning, tool execution, and LLM turn events separately.","url":"https://example.test/flue/reasoning-streams"}
</conversation>

This is the PREFIX of a turn that was too large to keep. The SUFFIX (recent work) is retained.

Summarize the prefix to provide context for the retained suffix:

## Original Request
[What did the user ask for in this turn?]

## Early Progress
- [Key decisions and work done in the prefix]

## Context for Suffix
- [Information needed to understand the retained recent work]

Be concise. Focus on what's needed to understand the kept suffix.
